title: guarea glabra subsp. excelsa
intro: Guarea glabra subsp. excelsa, also known as the West African mahogany or African mahogany, is a subspecies of Guarea glabra, a plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Guarea glabra subsp. excelsa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable resource for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Guarea glabra subsp. excelsa may include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
